title: Centralised Dealer
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Dealer on our Centralised Dealing Desk to join Davy within our Wealth Management division. Reporting to the Head of Dealing, the successful candidate will play a key role in the execution, oversight, and governance of trading activity, ensuring high standards of accuracy, efficiency, and compliance. **Key Responsibilities** * Order Management: Ensure the timely and accurate execution of all manual orders and monitor automated order flows in line with Best Execution requirements. * FX Execution: Execute foreign exchange trades efficiently and accurately in adherence to Best Execution policy. * Relationship Building: Maintain and develop strong working relationships with colleagues across Wealth Management and Davy Capital Markets as well as external stakeholders. * Governance \& Compliance: Adhere to all procedures and processes in accordance with Governance, Compliance, and Risk Management standards. * Trading Support: Act as the primary point of contact for trading\-related queries across Davy including market commentary, execution strategy, settlement, risk management, system operations, error resolution and corporate actions. * Operational Continuity: Ensure a smooth handover to the evening dealer to maintain seamless trading operations and be available on call in the evening if needed. * System Testing: Collaborate with the IS testing team on system upgrades, including the review and execution of test scripts. * Holiday Cover: Provide dealer cover during holiday periods, including Bank Holidays, to ensure order books are monitored effectively. * Training: Provide training for internal dealers preparing for evening shifts as well as new joiners. * Portfolio Rebalancing: Support the coordination and execution of investment selection portfolio rebalancing trades with internal investment teams and external specialised brokers while managing associated risks. * Performance Reporting: Contribute to relevant management information (MI) to monitor and measure trading performance. * Market Insights: Contribute to twice\-weekly market news summary sessions covering global economic and equity news within the Wealth Operations department. **Requirements** **About You** We are seeking a candidate with the following experience and capabilities: * A minimum of 5 years' experience in a similar role * In\-depth knowledge of trading operations, including trade lifecycles, post trade workflows and trading terminologies * Ability to work independently with minimal guidance * A Business or Finance degree (or equivalent) * Knowledge of Figaro, Infront, Bloomberg EMSX \& Fidessa is a distinct advantage * QFA qualification essential * Strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy * Ability to prioritise workload and work effectively under pressure * Excellent organisational, communication and time\-management skills * A positive attitude, strong work ethic, and high level of enthusiasm **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** The position set out above is defined as a "Controlled Function (CF)" role, namely CF4 under Section 20 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and Schedule 1 of the Regulations (S.I. No. 437 of 2011\) as amended. In accordance with Section 21 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and the Fitness and Probity Standards 2014, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Fitness \& Probity requirements, applicable to this position. The role is also subject to the Minimum Competency Code and therefore there is a requirement to hold or be willing to take the APA (Regulation \& Investment) or (Pensions) or Registered Stockbroker or QFA exams. Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Chef de Partie
description: **Job Ref:** OCC3642**Branch:** The Alex Hotel**Location:** The Alex Hotel Dublin, Dublin**Salary/Benefits:** Competitive Salary, Free Meal, Birthday Off**Contract type:** Permanent**Hours:** Full Time**Shift pattern:** Varied**Hours per week:** 37\.5**Posted date:** 11/05/2026**Closing date:** 13/06/2026 **About Us:** O'Callaghan Collection is a thoughtfully curated and design led collection of 5 Irish family\-owned and managed luxury boutique hotels located in the heart of Dublin and Gibraltar city centres. Therefore, our approach to attracting and retaining talent is centred around creating a workplace culture that values and supports our staff. In addition to investing in our employees' career development, we also prioritise their well\-being and work\-life balance. We offer competitive compensation packages, flexible scheduling, and a range of employee benefits and perks. Our goal is to attract and retain top talent in the industry who share our commitment to excellence and passion for providing exceptional service to our guests. If this sounds like you, we’d love to connect. We are Caring\|Proud\|Accountable\|Determined. **Benefits:** * Subsidized membership at our fully equipped gym * Your Birthday as an extra paid day off! * Enhanced holidays with service * Rewards programme * Refer a friend \- up to €1000 reward * Discounted room stays \- friends and family * Discounted FB when you are a guest in any of our hotels * The opportunity to progress \- 4 Hotels in Dublin city and a Hotel in Gibraltar * Tax saver for travel tickets * Bike to work scheme and a safe place to leave your bike. **Role information:** * Food Preparation: Prepare ingredients, cook, and plate dishes according to the menu and chef's specifications, maintaining consistency and presentation. * Training and Supervision: Train junior kitchen staff, monitor their performance, and ensure adherence to safety and hygiene standards. * Inventory Control: Manage inventory for the assigned section, ensuring proper stock levels, minimizing wastage, and reporting shortages. * Quality Control: Maintain high standards of food quality, taste, and appearance, conducting regular checks and making adjustments as needed. * Collaboration: Coordinate with other chefs and kitchen staff to ensure smooth service, communicate effectively, and assist in menu planning and development. **Requirements:** * Proven experience as a chef de partie and or culinary qualification. * Haccp training. * Acknowledge the pivotal purpose of health and safety protocols in a hotel environment. * Provide a superb guest service and have excellent people skills. * Excellent attention to detail in delivering customer care standards. * Be able to work as part of a team and on own initiative. * Be available and flexible for early starts and late finishes, weekends and bank holidays. Dublin’s most stylish hotel, The Alex is a newly refurbished sophisticated hotel packed full of stunning design and clever features to ensure you have a comfortable stay, whether you’re with us for a few hours or a few days. Our location and meeting facilities make The Alex a stylish choice for events of all sizes, while our unique and well\-serviced work hub featuring Dublin’s fastest WiFi (1Gb) offers a comfortable space for you to catch up on some work over a delicious coffee from our café. Our guests can also enjoy access to a 24\-hour gym, dine in a stunning restaurant featuring delicious dishes and a varied cocktail menu and experience a perfect night’s sleep in our designer bedrooms. We are consistently ranking high on TripAdvisor for our exceptional service and unforgettable guest experiences!

title: Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Specialists with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that train cutting\-edge AI models to think like expert scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working chemists and researchers who want to apply their deep domain knowledge in a high\-impact, forward\-looking way. No AI background required — just serious chemistry expertise and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve complex, real\-world chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated chemistry content for scientific accuracy, depth, and clarity * Craft challenging problem statements that push AI reasoning to graduate\-level standards * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of scientific rigor and precision * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one of: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with technical concepts at a graduate or research level * Naturally detail\-oriented with strong written communication skills in English * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently * Experience coding in Python or using computational chemistry tools (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) is a strong plus **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, content evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user * Background in academic research, scientific publishing, or curriculum development * Experience designing problem sets, exams, or instructional materials at a graduate level **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Apply your advanced chemistry knowledge in a meaningful, intellectually stimulating way * Freelance autonomy with the structure of impactful, task\-based work * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Cleaner - Naas

title: Client Onboarding Analyst II
description: **Work Location** : Dublin, Ireland **Hours** 35 **Line Of Business** TD Securities **Pay Details** We’re committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to all our colleagues. As a candidate, we encourage you to have an open dialogue with a member of our HR Team and ask compensation related questions, including pay details for this role. **Job Description** Global Operations and Business Services employees are unique individuals whose varied talents and experiences bring value to the way they deliver for the franchise. Whether you are analytical or creative, business savvy or a people person, Global Operations and Business Services provides an ideal opportunity to succeed. The Global Operations and Business Services team is a diverse and dynamic group of people delivering continued business success through leadership, collaboration, innovation and visionary thinking. We provide critical infrastructure services that support and/or control the trading, investment and corporate banking functions of TD Securities. Within our core values, we foster a culture of diversity, inclusion and community support/giving. **Team Overview** Global Business Services \& Operations is at the core of TD Securities. We are a dynamic, multi\-faceted division that partners with all parts of the Dealer, including Sales \& Trading to provide banking, securities, and asset management services to clients globally. Global Business Services \& Operations recruits highly motivated individuals who can help the Dealer and our clients facilitate business. Professionals in Global Business Services \& Operations come from a variety of academic and professional backgrounds. The successful candidate will be part of the Client Management team within Global Business Services \& Operations. The team's primary responsibility is relationship management, coordination and management of onboarding of clients for TD Securities, globally. Client Management is also responsible for assisting with large client relationships initiatives that impact the dealer. The Client Management team is committed to ensuring services provided across the Bank and to its clients are delivered in a professional manner, that risk is minimised, and the client has a positive experience. The candidate is expected to think creatively, improve the daily process, and take responsibility and ownership for this to happen.. **Job Description/Accountabilities** * Has a solid understanding of the client onboarding requirements including the new global regulatory framework, credit, legal and AML/KYC, FATCA, CRS, MiFID and understands the role of various stakeholders involved * Working with sales and trading to grow long term relationships with buy side clients as they organically grow with TD * Proactively working with buy side clients to assess needs and priorities daily * Assist the desk and stakeholders in managing priorities * Expediting the ability to trade whilst ensuring all required stakeholders are engaged and risks minimized. * Participate in new client and product strategies for the Dealer globally, offering value\-added ideas and strategies * Work with the Front Office and internal stakeholders daily to understand the client needs and track and prioritize high value relationships * Working with stakeholders to understand regulatory and documentation requirements for large global institutional clients * Building and maintaining strong relationships with internal stakeholders * Maintain client status updates current in the database to track and monitor workflow * Escalate and manage client issues proactively and in a timely manner Job Requirements * Undergraduate degree/ college diploma * Up to 3 years' experience of working experience in either buy side firms or Capital Markets industry * Understanding of Financial Markets and trading products * Proven ability to maintain and manage large client relationships * Demonstrates time management skills and commitment to delivering results within aggressive timelines and ability to support global desks * Ability to analyze, organize and prioritize work while meeting multiple deadlines * Excellent communication and presentation skills to share recommendations with colleagues * High degree of intellectual curiosity, passion, and self\-motivation * Ability to work independently and within a team environment experience * Ability to work within a diverse, global workforce that is client service oriented * Ability to understand and find appropriate applications for data and analysis results * Meticulous attention to detail and due diligence * Sound understanding of risk processes and methodologies * Demonstrates a high aptitude in time management * Design, creativity, and problem\-solving skills * Fast learner who is dedicated to continuously improving their skills and expertise. * Strong OUTLOOK/EXCEL/POWERPOINT skills **Who We Are** TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities. **Our Total Rewards Package** Our Total Rewards package reflects the investment we make in our colleagues to help them, and their families achieve their well\-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary and several other key plans such as health and well\-being benefits, including medical coverage, paid time off, career development, and reward and recognition programs. **Additional Information** We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home. **Colleague Development** If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities. If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact. We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best. **Training \& Onboarding** We will provide training and onboarding sessions to ensure that you’ve got everything you need to succeed in your new role. **Interview Process** We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call. **Accommodation** If you require an accommodation for the recruitment / interview process (including alternate formats of materials, or accessible meeting rooms or other accommodation), please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Client Service Executive to join Davy. In the role, the successful candidate will: * The provision of general administrative support to a team of Directors, Associate Directors and Senior Private Client Advisers. * Acting as the first point of contact to clients; dealing promptly and efficiently with client's queries. * Preparation of investment presentations and collation of documentation for client meetings; auditing customer relationship management system to ensure records and supporting documents are accurate \& compliant in preparation of client meetings. * Management of follow up activities from client meetings; completion of minutes of meetings and monitoring completion of tasks. * Updating and monitoring the CRM system on an ongoing basis; preparing documentation to comply with legislative requirements in data management for our clients * Facilitate client requests by liaising with internal departments to ensure operational procedures and compliance obligations are met executing tasks; acting as the primary point of contact with all internal departments. * Managing reporting and operation of tasks in conjunction with other team members; implementing and communicating company practice in carrying out tasks. * Liaising with Tax Advisers, Solicitors, Brokers and Life Offices on behalf of servicing our clients. * Assist with administrative tasks involved with planning and preparation of various client events and conferences; provide on\-site support and hospitality at such events. Occasional out of office hours. * Participate in interdepartmental projects to identify best practice to enhance servicing our clients; facilitate deployment and communication of new applications to the team. * Working with the team on the completion and follow up of Investor Policy Statement. * Contribute in company campaign workshops. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * A minimum of 1 year of experience working in an administrative position; * Degree or Masters level in Business/Finance related area; * A high level of proficiency in MS Word, Excel, Outlook and PowerPoint; * A flexible, positive attitude towards work * Excellent attention to detail; * An ability to work effectively as part of a team; * A professional telephone manner; * Excellent organisational skills; * An ability to prioritise own workload. **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. * Health and Wellbeing Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. * Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. * Growth and Development Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. * Positive Workplace Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. * Values and Culture Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Client Support to Recruitment Specialist – Time to Accelerate!
description: **Client Support to Recruitment Specialist – Time to Accelerate!** * **Fast\-Track Training:** Everything you need to transition into Recruitment quickly. * **Career Clarity:** A transparent roadmap with a defined path to promotion. * **Results\-Driven Success:** No "waiting in line": performance dictates your promotion speed. Currently in a client support role and ready to step up? **Archer** is growing and seeking exceptionally driven professionals to join our team. You will move beyond support to drive the recruitment delivery process, stepping directly in front of our partnered clients to make a tangible impact. At Archer, we specialise in **IT Recruitment** , partnering with market leaders across the Med\-Tech and Financial Services sectors. We deliver top\-tier IT talent and build out high\-performing teams across Dublin and Europe. As we continue to scale, we want you to join our growth mission. **Why Join Archer:** * **Fast\-Track Training:** Transition from support to specialist with 1:1 coaching, mentorship, and access to over 200 training modules. Become a specialist in less than 12 months. * **Career Clarity:** Eliminate ambiguity with a structured roadmap. Our promotion path takes you from Associate to Lead in less than 3 years. * **Results\-Driven Success:** You are in the driver’s seat. You dictate your progression and earnings\-no more waiting in line for your turn to move up. **Why Become a Recruiter:** * **Ownership:** Take full control of the recruitment delivery process and own your outcomes. * **Incentives:** High\-performance rewards including uncapped commission, bonuses, and international trips. * **Client Impact:** Become the go\-to partner for major firms by delivering exceptional results. **What You Will Have:** * Ideally, **3\+ years** of professional experience in Client Support, Customer Support, or a similar role. * A valid **EU Passport or Stamp 4 Visa** . * A desire to thrive in an **office\-based role** (Monday–Friday) in the heart of Dublin. **Compensation (Base \+ Commission \+ Bonus):** * **Year 1:** €47,000\+ * **Year 2:** €70,000\+ * **Year 3:** €100,000\+ * *Base salary (DOE): Early\-to\-mid €30k range \+ Uncapped Commission.* For more information, contact **Saoirse Lawton** at **saoirse.lawton@archer.ie** or call **\+353 (0\)1 649 8517** .

title: Climate Data Specialist
description: **Climate Data Specialist (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your expertise in climate modeling, ecology, or environmental science could directly shape how AI reasons about one of the most important challenges facing our planet? We're looking for Climate Data Specialists — environmental scientists with advanced degrees — to develop complex, real\-world problems that train cutting\-edge AI models to think more rigorously about climate and environmental science. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for researchers and domain experts who want to apply their deep scientific knowledge to frontier AI development. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design, solve, and review advanced environmental science problems with genuine real\-world relevance * Apply your expertise in climate modeling, ecology, sustainability, or related disciplines to construct complex, rigorous problem sets * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to sharpen AI model reasoning * Evaluate the scientific accuracy, depth, and clarity of AI\-generated outputs in your area of expertise * Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of scientific rigor and communication quality **Who You Are** * Holds a Master's or PhD in Environmental Science, Climate Science, Ecology, or a closely related field * Experienced with Python or R for research or data analysis * Deep subject matter expertise in climate modeling, ecology, sustainability, or environmental systems * Exceptionally clear written communicator — able to explain complex concepts with precision * Detail\-oriented, methodical, and comfortable working independently in an asynchronous environment * Fluent in English **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, data quality assurance, or AI evaluation workflows * Background in interdisciplinary environmental research spanning multiple subfields * Familiarity with AI tools or large language models as an end user * Experience designing or grading advanced academic problem sets **Why Join Us** * Work at the frontier of AI development alongside leading research labs * Fully remote and asynchronous — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure and consistency of ongoing project\-based work * Apply your scientific expertise to problems that matter at a global scale * Contribute to AI systems that will reason about climate and environmental science for years to come * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Clinical Monitoring Analyst (Cluepoints) - Remote
description: This unique role within IQVIA lends itself to a Lead programmer on Cluepoints called Centralized Monitoring Analyst (CMA) with strong communication and organisation skills. Having experience with SAS programming is necessary. This role is part of a sponsor dedicated project where you will be working with the client directly to manage deliveries. The CMA contributes to the execution of the Centralized Monitoring process by performing the setup, programming and refresh(es) of the Centralized Monitoring platform, according to the study\-specific Centralized Monitoring Plan (CMP). **Main Responsibilities Include** * Supports the Centralized Monitoring Lead (CML) in the development of the fit\-for purpose CM strategy by contributing to the identification of the relevant CM elements to be implemented for the study (Quality Tolerance Limits (QTL), Key Risk Indicators, data visualizations and Data Quality Assessments) * Setup/Program QTL, KRI and Data Quality Assessments according to the study CMP * Refresh the Centralized Monitoring Platform at predefined frequency to enable signal identification * Generate and maintain the Centralized Monitoring Platform specifications document and file it in eTMF * Review and understanding of protocol is key * Understand the data sources for the study, the data transfer specifications and the data structure * Execute and document peer\-review of CM platform setup done by other CMA colleagues **Experience** * At least 3 years relevant experience in the area of pharmaceutical research and development or related field preferably in centralized Monitoring, data management, and/or biostatistics * 2\-3 years SAS experience including macro language * Knowledge/ experience working on Cluepoints * Ability to work in an autonomous way and engage with relevant stakeholders to execute activities related to study level centralized monitoring process. * Ability to understand basic statistical analysis concepts and to interpret their outcome. * Ability to maintain confidentiality of data and information during interactions with staff at all levels * Ability to work with limited close supervision. * Demonstrated communication, interpersonal, organizational and problem\-solving skills * High levels of initiative, drive and commitment. * Prior experience in a Data Management related field is required * Experience with relational databases, preferably Clinical Data Management and EDC Systems * Experience communicating with all levels of personnel and participating in collaborative work team (locally and globally). * Advanced knowledge of federal regulations, Good Clinical Practice, and Good Clinical Data Management Practices. * Excellent command, both verbal and written, of English **This role is not eligible for UK visa sponsorship** IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

title: Clinical Nurse Manger 1 –Neonatal and Neonatal High Dependency Units
description: **Purpose of the Role** The purpose of this post is: * To provide clinical and managerial leadership to a designated nursing team and ensure the provision of high quality nursing care to infants and families within the neonatal high dependency area and ward cots on the Neonatal Unit. * To assist in the safe, effective and efficient management of the department within allocated resources and to undertake delegated responsibility for the department in the absence of the CNM2\. * To be a role model in coordinating and managing the specialist neonatal care required for those complex, medical, surgical and preterm neonates within the 6 bed Neonatal High Dependency Unit (NHDU). * To safeguard the provision of high standards of quality care to infants and their families consistent with the mission, vision, values and strategic plan of the hospital. **Eligibility Criteria, Qualifications And Experience** * Be registered on the Children’s Division and/or the General Division of the Register of Nurses \& Midwives maintained by the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland; * Have 2 years post graduate neonatal experience; * Have proven clinical and managerial ability, leadership, communication and organisational skills; * Demonstrate evidence of continuing professional development at the appropriate level. **How To Apply \& Informal Enquiries** Applications for this post must be accompanied by a cover letter, setting out relevant experience that illustrates how the essential criteria listed above is met. The criterion for short listing is based on the requirements of the post, as outlined in the eligibility criteria. * Please note that you must submit a cover letter with your CV, this forms part of your application and CV’s will not be accepted without a detailed cover letter. The closing date for submissions of CV’s and cover letter is **Monday, 25th of May 2026 by 11:45pm.** Applications must be completed through the advertised post on **CHI.jobs** by clicking **‘Apply for Job’.** **Applications will not be accepted through direct email or any other method.** For informal enquiries for this specialty/department, please contact Jenny Dunne, CNM3 Neonatology, Jenny.Dunne1@childrenshealthireland.ie, Elizabeth Sweeney, CNM3 Neonatology Elizabeth.sweeney@childrenshealthireland.ieor ADON for CDC\- Deirdre Hughes, Deirdre.Hughes@childrenshealthireland.ie For other queries relating to this recruitment process, please contact Talent Acquisition Specialist, Evgeniya.Byvakina@childrenshealthireland.ie **PLEASE NOTE:** CHI has transitioned to a process of a one commencement day per month for all new employees and Secondments. This update to our Onboarding process is aligned to changes in our monthly/fortnightly payroll and with the corporate induction program. This process enhancement ensures that we can thoroughly prepare for your arrival and facilitate a smooth transition in your onboarding journey. **It is important for you to note that if you do not have your pre\-employments and mandatory training completed in time, your commencement date will be deferred to the next available date.** Below, you’ll find the list of **commencement dates for 2026** for your information. * 11th May * 8th June * 6th July * 10th August * 7th September * 5th October * 2nd November

title: Cloud Security Engineer
description: Cloud Security Engineer, Dublin/Hybrid Permanent Talentspot has partnered on an exclusive basis with global, market\-leading, enterprise\-scale end\-user as they increase the knowledge and add further Cloud Security firepower to their technology security and risk community that consists of around 50 colleagues globally. Opportunity Summary In this new position, they are seeking an experienced Cloud Security Engineer. This role offers exciting opportunities for growth and impact as this organisation scales their business and continues to innovate. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: * Design, implement, and maintain security controls across cloud environments * Secure cloud infrastructure, platforms, and services that support internally developed and externally facing applications * Partner closely with Infrastructure, DevOps, Engineering, and business teams * Ensure cloud security controls align with industry best practices and regulatory requirements * Proactively identify emerging risks and evaluate new technologies * Contribute to shaping their long\-term cloud security strategy Role \& Responsibilities: * Design and implement secure cloud architectures across public cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP * Develop and enforce cloud security standards, guardrails, and policies using infrastructure as code and policy as code frameworks * Monitor cloud environments for misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, and active threats * Implement and manage identity and access management controls, including least privilege access and role\-based access control * Partner with DevOps and Engineering teams to integrate security into CI/CD pipelines and cloud native workflows * Deploy and manage cloud security tools such as CSPM, CWPP, container security platforms, and cloud workload monitoring solutions * Conduct cloud security assessments, architecture reviews, and risk evaluations * Support incident response activities related to cloud environments, including investigation and remediation * Ensure compliance with relevant regulatory requirements and industry frameworks * Maintain awareness of evolving cloud threats and emerging security technologies Skills \& Experience * Come from an employment background in enterprise\-scale, global, complex, public cloud security * Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field * Proven experience as a Cloud Security Engineer or in a similar, cloud\-focused security role * Extensive hands\-on experience with major cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP * Deep understanding of cloud networking, architecture principles, and shared responsibility models * Experience with infrastructure as code tools such as Terraform or CloudFormation * Familiarity with containerisation and orchestration technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes * Experience implementing cloud security controls, including encryption, logging, monitoring, and identity management * Knowledge of regulatory requirements and frameworks such as NIST, ISO 27001, CIS benchmarks, and SOC 2 * Experience with DevSecOps practices and integrating security into automated deployment pipelines * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills in complex cloud environments * Comfortable working collaboratively as part of a global team * Ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, and solve complex problems with initiative and drive 2 Ability to communicate effectively in both business and technical settings * Strategic thinker with an innovative mindset and strong attention to detail You must be eligible to live and work in Ireland with no restrictions, as sponsorship now or in the future is not available.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)
description: **Cloud Solutions Architect** **Daily Rate Contract (1 day onsite)** My Dublin based client is seeking an experienced Cloud Solutions Architect for a daily rate contract. The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * Ensure solutions meet performance, reliability, and governance standards **Required Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience designing and implementing scalable cloud solutions * Strong background in modern cloud\-native architecture (microservices, serverless, containerisation) * Hands\-on Infrastructure as Code expertise (Terraform, CloudFormation) * Deep experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS or Azure) * Experience designing or implementing Kubernetes solutions (preferably EKS or equivalent) * Proven ability to manage and optimise public cloud infrastructure environments * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Experience producing clear, structured architecture and design documentation **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to regulated or complex enterprise environments * Experience across both AWS and Azure * CI/CD and DevOps implementation using IaC tooling * Experience supporting cloud migration or transformation programmes **Certifications (Advantageous)** * AWS Certified Solutions Architect (or equivalent cloud certification)

title: Commercial & Technology Lawyer
description: We are working with a market\-leading international law firm who are seeking an Associate Solicitor to join their Data \& Technology team within their Dublin office. **The Role** The successful candidate will join a globally recognised Commercial practice, advising the firm’s international and domestic client base on a broad range of commercial and technology\-related matters. The candidate will have the opportunity to work on some of the most high\-profile and challenging commercial contracts within the jurisdiction. The candidate’s role will also involve supporting colleagues on issues including GDPR compliance and investigations, emerging technologies, IP and data aspects of joint ventures and mergers \& acquisitions. The candidate will also demonstrate a willingness to participate in business development initiatives, while maintaining strong relationships with the firm’s client base. **Essential Criteria** * 3\-6 PQE; * Strong background in commercial law and technology within a medium to large commercial firm; * Ability to demonstrate strong commercial awareness; * Outstanding academics; * Technically excellent; * Negotiation skills; * Willingness to become involved in business development; * Ability to work independently and as a team. **Benefits** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Generous benefits package; * Extensive opportunities for career development; * Excellent client base; * Hybrid working; * Inclusive and friendly culture. Please contact Nuala Darragh on \+44 7540 310 755 or email nuala.darragh@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: Commercial Analyst
description: **The Role** ION is looking for smart and ambitious candidates to join our Commercial Team which leads engagement with customers and prospective customers. Successful people in ION will be able to learn from previous transactions experience and apply going forward to improve the process. As you learn ION’s approach, you will become an ambassador, with opportunities to coach new hires and staff in our portfolio companies. As part of a highly motivated team, your role will be pivotal in the management of ION’s commercial and legal relationships with our customers. You will be involved in every aspect of customer relations and channel that knowledge into cogent positioning to help close sales transactions. Key Responsibilities * Analyse / reconcile existing contracts and develop proposed renewal options * Closely interact with senior management to construct and deliver customer commercial documents * Develop relationships with ION Product / business areas to facilitate the ION sales process * Support the integration of newly acquired companies into the ION sales process * Partner with senior management and legal team in the negotiation of customer contracts * Drive and execute commercial operational efficiencies within the commercial team and wider sales organisation * Support the account management team in forming and nurturing customer relations * Assist in management of the daily commercial proposal workflow **Required Skills, Experience And Qualifications** * Commercially astute * Attention to detail * Ability to work under pressure and to deadlines * Intellectually curious * Strong team player, self\-motivated and hard\-working individual who is goal orientated * Strong analytical skills * Ability to multi\-task and adapt to a rapidly changing environment * Good interpersonal and communication (verbal and written) skills * Good knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint * 1\.1 or 2\.1 degree * 1\+ years in experience in a fast\-paced environment **About Us** We’re a diverse group of visionary innovators who provide trading and workflow automation software, high\-value analytics, and strategic consulting to corporations, central banks, financial institutions, and governments. Founded in 1999, we’ve achieved tremendous growth by bringing together some of the best and most successful financial technology companies in the world. * Over 2,000 of the world’s leading corporations, including 50% of the Fortune 500 and 30% of the world’s central banks, trust ION solutions to manage their cash, in\-house banking, commodity supply chain, trading and risk. * Over 800 of the world’s leading banks and broker\-dealers use our electronic trading platforms to operate the world’s financial market infrastructure. ION is a rapidly expanding and dynamic group with 13,000 employees and offices in more than 40 cities around the globe. Our ever\-expanding global footprint, cutting edge products, and over 40,000 customers worldwide provide an unparalleled career experience for those who share our vision. ION is committed to maintaining a supportive and inclusive environment for people with diverse backgrounds and experiences. We respect the varied identities, abilities, cultures, and traditions of the individuals who comprise our organization and recognize the value that different backgrounds and points of view bring to our business. ION adheres to an equal employment opportunity policy that prohibits discriminatory practices or harassment against applicants or employees based on any legally impermissible factor.

title: Commercial Analyst
description: **Position Description** The Flexibility Market Design team is part of the DMSO Design pillar of DMSO (Distribution Markets \& System Operation). The DMSO Design pillar is formerly known as ‘The National Network, Local Connections Programme which was established to deliver the capabilities needed in ESB Networks (people, processes and systems) to change how the electricity distribution system is developed and managed. The Clean Energy Package sets out that “All consumers should be able to benefit from directly participating in the market,” A core objective of the DMSO Design function is to develop integrated designs for ESB Networks’ DMSO organisation. This is an exciting and transformative area, and we are seeking applications from individuals who are interested in playing an active role in establishing these new capabilities in our organisation and our industry. The commercial analyst role is part of the Flexibility Market Design team in the DMSO Design pillar. This team is responsible for setting the development of the appropriate market\-based solutions to enable customers to contribute directly and benefit from the transformation in the distribution network. Through flexible services, customers will be able to offer their capabilities in demand side response, generation or storage to ESB Networks as DSO to efficiently and securely manage the distribution network. The team is responsible for: * Leading the development of flexibility market rules. * Leading the development of new flexibility products in which customers such as demand response, aggregators, generation and storage can participate in via the flexibility market. * Managing existing and future flexibility market contracts such as, but not limited to, validation and settlement of flexibility services. This is done using a Flexibility Services Tracker which will be replaced by a Flexibility Market System over the next 18 months. * Developing appropriate commercial models to value the flexibility products and designing appropriate incentives for customers. * Establishing an appropriate contractual model through which ESB Networks procures and financially settles these flexible services. * Developing the appropriate processes through which customers can participate in transmission services and wholesale markets. **Key Responsibilities** * Engaging in the settlement and validation process for the flexible service providers (customers) in the various flexibility products and services. * Analysis of market data and outcomes to inform flexible service cost forecast * Working with Market Operations Lead in management of the flexible service contracts during commercial operation * Analyse the necessary operational and financial information to prepare performance statements for customers operating within flexible service contracts * Working closely with the Account Holder for each flexible service provider * Involved in developing improvements to the settlement tool and testing of same * Feeding into market process mapping sessions and development of same * Support the development of enduring operational tools such as Flexibility market system for procuring and settling services by contributing to requirements gathering and end user testing * Support the development of the market design and processes (including registration, qualification, competition frameworks and validation and settlement) for participating in flexible services, in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders. * Work closely with other teams to monitor expenditure and support financial projection of flexible service costs. * Supporting development of tender documentation for the individual flexible services and initiatives. **Knowledge, Skills And Experience** Essential * A primary degree in Economics, Finance, or related discipline or equivalent work experience in a commercial role. * 3\+ years relevant experience required * Strong analytical skills * Strong commercial awareness * Good interpersonal skills and ability to work with cross functional teams * Excellent written and communication skills * Excellent Microsoft Excel skills Desirable * Understanding of commercial/contractual risk management * Knowledge of existing Irish or European electricity arrangements. * Knowledge of Procurement rules * Knowledge of probabilistic or statistical modelling techniques * Data analytics capability using Power BI or other data analytics package **Location** Flexible with minimum one anchor day in Finglas. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Reporting To** Flexibility Market Design Manager, Distribution Markets \& System Operation (DMSO) Design. **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €49,000 to €56,000 per annum **Closing Date** 25th April 2026

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Belgian Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **FY27 Grad Program Job Description \- Sales Path** **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Sales Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Sales** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Dutch What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. title: Commercial Real Estate Lawyer
description: Matheson’s Commercial Real Estate team is one of the leading commercial property law practices in Ireland, acting in the largest and most high profile real estate transactions occurring in the Irish market. We are hiring experienced Commercial Real Estate lawyers to join our team to work directly with our international client base on complex and challenging commercial real estate transactions. At Matheson we invest in our people, and you will receive excellent mentoring directly from Partners as well as having the opportunity to mentor and coach junior solicitors. We also offer a firm wide, tailored learning and development programme at all PQE levels giving you the opportunity to develop strong business and practice management skills and network with peers across the firm. **About the opportunity** This is an opportunity to join a busy and growing commercial real estate team in a responsible and time sensitive role. **About the role** * You will be involved in the acquisition, development, asset management structuring and onward sale and leasing of large scale developments for the leading and most innovative developers in the market place; * You will be working directly with our international client base on some of the largest, complex and challenging commercial real estate transactions; * You will be involved in the negotiation of commercial leases and agreement for leases for both landlords and tenants; * You will be involved in the commercial real estate aspects of corporate transactions, including the sale and purchase of both private and public companies; * You will be advising owner/occupiers on the acquisition of office, industrial and manufacturing premises; and * You will be involved in the acquisition, disposal and financing of single assets, asset portfolios and loan portfolios. **The person** * You are an experienced lawyer with a strong track record in commercial real estate matters; * You have deep technical expertise, are highly client focused and committed to consistently exceeding client expectations; * You can manage a diverse workload, deliver quality, commercially\-focused advice with minimal supervision, and lead and develop junior lawyers; * You enjoy the law you practice, are hardworking and resilient and will thrive in a diverse and inclusive environment where you will work in partnership with top legal talent in a market leading team that is continuing to grow; * You will enjoy establishing relationships with a diverse range of clients and colleagues; and * You are enthusiastic about business development, building long\-term, meaningful client relationships and contributing to the growth of the practice. **Why join Matheson** At Matheson you have the opportunity to work with exceptional people and a broad range of high calibre domestic and international clients. Working at Matheson provides you with an environment where you can thrive by living our values of Partnership, Respect, Innovation, Diversity and Entrepreneurship. Our Impactful Business Programme is delivered by a number of committees which drive key initiatives around D\&I, CSR, Environmental Sustainability and the Arts. We are also a founding signatory of the Pro Bono Pledge Ireland. We have a hybrid working policy. We provide an excellent working environment, a competitive benefits package and the opportunity to engage in a range of learning and development initiatives. We are proud to support the Mindful Business Charter. *Accessibility: If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience.* *We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Compliance Analyst
description: **About us:** Pelagos Insurance Capital is a global specialty insurance and reinsurance company focused on creating value through strategic capital allocation, expert risk selection, and a network of long\-term underwriting partnerships . We have built a strong foundation for scale and profitable growth, underpinned by our disciplined approach to risk selection and our financial strength, which is reflected in our insurer financial strength ratings of A from AM Best, A\- from S\&P and A3 from Moody’s. Our network of underwriting partners and our highly diversified portfolio enable us to proactively navigate market cycles, offer innovative and tailored solutions, capitalize on favorable risk\-reward opportunities, and produce superior returns for shareholder **s. title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Compliance Analyst \- Surveillance** A leading financial services organisation in Dublin is seeking a Compliance Analyst to join their dedicated Compliance Department. * Join a well\-established financial services organisation in Dublin with a strong reputation for nurturing talent and providing comprehensive training opportunities. * Play a key role in maintaining regulatory standards by conducting email reviews, supporting investigations, and enhancing compliance policies within a supportive team environment. **What you'll do:** * Conduct thorough reviews of email communications for trading groups, ensuring all correspondence adheres to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Perform detailed searches related to internal investigations, collaborating closely with colleagues across departments to gather relevant information. * Prepare comprehensive weekly and monthly memos summarising findings from email surveillance reviews, presenting clear insights to senior management. * Assist in the development and enhancement of compliance policies and procedures, adapting them to reflect changes in business operations, regulations, and emerging issues. * Stay up\-to\-date with relevant regulatory developments and changes, proactively sharing knowledge with the wider team to ensure collective understanding. * Participate actively in other compliance\-related projects as needed, demonstrating flexibility and willingness to support organisational goals. * Work collaboratively with colleagues across departments to build effective working relationships that facilitate smooth project delivery. * Utilise technological applications and tools efficiently to streamline compliance processes and improve overall department productivity. * Contribute positively to a culture of continuous improvement by identifying areas for process optimisation within the compliance function. **What you bring:** * Bachelor’s degree (preferably in Business, Finance, Economics or related field) with at least three years’ experience in compliance roles within financial services. * Prior experience at a Registered Investment Adviser or Broker\-Dealer is highly desirable for this position. * Strong understanding of regulatory agencies such as Central Bank of Ireland, FCA, SEC, CFTC, MAS along with familiarity with key regulations including Investment Advisers Act 1940, Securities Act 1933, Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player who thrives on co\-operation and collaboration within diverse groups. * Ability to prioritise tasks effectively in environments characterised by constant change and evolving demands. * Entrepreneurial mindset combined with proven ability to manage projects independently while remaining responsive to team needs. * Exceptional writing skills paired with analytical abilities that enable you to produce clear reports and solve complex problems. * Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint as well as other technological applications relevant to compliance work. * Broad knowledge of financial products and the wider financial services industry is considered advantageous for this role. * Strong interpersonal skills enabling you to build dependable relationships across departments.
