title: HubSpot Solutions Strategist
description: Salted Stone is a fast\-paced, full\-service digital marketing agency, and we are looking for a **HubSpot Solutions Strategist** to join our growing team. Our mission is to empower growth\-minded companies through creative solutions combined with smart technology. We believe nothing is impossible and always solve for the customer. We aim to attract, engage, and develop exceptional people and, in turn, deliver distinctive, lasting improvements for our customers. This is a mid\-level full\-time position reporting to the Director of Solutions on our Solutions Architecture team (commonly known as Technical Marketing elsewhere). The HubSpot Solutions Strategist will help our clients develop, implement, and optimize new technologies and processes that tie together the entire customer lifecycle using the HubSpot platform. The HubSpot Solutions Strategist supports the solutions team by cultivating professional expertise and applying this to address a range of technical marketing, sales, and service issues. They determine appropriate courses of action based on established guidelines and adapt processes and methods as necessary. Ideal candidates enjoy working directly with our clients and internal team to lead engagements from a technical standpoint (think: ideation up to execution), are organized, great communicators, and aren’t afraid to tackle challenges head\-on! **Job Responsibilities** * Complete and deliver all elements of the Salted Stones Solutions Team Product offerings (Data Migration, Portal Strategy, and HubSpot Onboarding including Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, etc.) * Interact directly with and provide a translation layer to technically\-oriented marketers and software developers, internally and on the client\-side. * Lead client\-facing calls, providing technical expertise and a complete understanding of tools to help clients understand and use offerings effectively. * Run solutions onboarding engagements (Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, and/or Service Hub) without relying on CS team members to run points/project management. * Work closely with the development team (both internally and externally) to connect existing systems to HubSpot. * Be able to generate a standard ERD for either existing or proposed integration structure. * Leverage understanding of data structures to provide recommendations and implementation of systems setup. * Be able to discuss 1st party, 3rd party, and Middleware integration solutions. * Troubleshoot integration and data problems. * Regularly thinks creatively on how to use the HubSpot Platform to solve for client needs. * Develop client\-facing supporting documentation and processes for new technology configurations, customizations, and functionalities. * Execute assigned tasks with a high degree of quality and on time. * Provide accurate time estimates for your work, work within timelines and budgets, and other applicable project management concerns. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the team by identifying areas for optimization, streamlining processes, and implementing innovative solutions. * Lead pre\-sales engagements from discovery through solution design, translating client business objectives into scalable, technically sound HubSpot strategies and clearly articulating value, approach, and scope to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Deliver tailored solution demonstrations that effectively articulate the value of HubSpot and proposed architectures, aligning platform capabilities to client\-specific requirements, use cases, and desired business outcomes. **Required Competencies** * Successful experience in a Solutions Strategy, Technical Marketing, Marketing/Revenue Technologist, Marketing/Revenue Operations, etc., role (agency experience a plus); ideally suited for early to mid\-career level, but not entry level. * Proven experience with HubSpot (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub). * Excellent organizational skills and the ability to drive and participate in multiple client engagements at once. * Strong technical writing skills \& documentation skills, primarily for creation of compelling client\-facing deliverables. * Ability to confidently communicate with clients as needed. * Excellent project management, organizational and time management skills; and you're comfortable managing multiple projects (budgets, timelines, personalities) simultaneously. * Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Analytics, Databox, and similar analytics tools. * Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Tag Manager. * Basic knowledge of marketing and sales automation tools, business intelligence tools, data enrichment tools, etc. * Strong understanding of sales processes and CRM lifecycle management (pipeline design, lead lifecycle, attribution basics) * Experience contributing to or supporting proposal/RFP responses and statements of work (SOWs). * Ability to lead structured discovery sessions, gathering and synthesizing business, technical, and data requirements. * Demonstrated consultative mindset, with the ability to challenge client assumptions and guide toward better solutions. **S2 General Requirements** * **Team player.** Friendly, authentic, collaborative, and makes an effort with relationships (the S2 team, clients, HubSpot). Shows care and respect for those around them and themselves. Live our Values. * **Growth mindset.** Prioritises learning and self\-improvement. Shows motivation and dedication to growth, personally and professionally. * **Agile** . Adapts to changing priorities and conditions, complexity, and change. * **Proactive** . Demonstrates tenacity and acts without being told what to do. Brings new ideas to S2\. Finds solutions and opportunities, not just problems. * **Prioritizes communication** \- Clear, transparent, and consistent with communication across your team and manager, with other teams, and with clients. If in doubt, over\-communicates. Isn’t afraid to call out and help correct mistakes before they impact the team or the business. * **HubSpot\-centric.** HubSpot is at the center of most work at Salted Stone. Not everyone has to be a HubSpot expert to work here, but everyone needs to have a baseline interest and understanding. **You’ll enjoy working:** In a hybrid role \- our team chose the balance of remote work and contact time in our Dublin office that best suits them and their roles. They’re split between Ireland and Perth, Australia so are used to doing things on Zoom and async. S2’s wider team is spread across North America, Australia, Ireland, and the Philippines so there may be times that we ask you to flex your schedule to allow for collaboration with different timezones. This flex goes both ways. **Benefits** Our Irish team enjoy: * Company paid health insurance and pension match * Birthday and Anniversary Leave (an additional paid day off) * Holisticly wellness benefit * Regular Culture Committee initiatives \& events * Cycle to Work Scheme * Flexible, Hybrid or Remote Work * Team Lunches \& Happy Hours * Company provided laptop **Application process** 1. Resume review 2. Initial call with the Recruitment Team 3. Full interview with the hiring Director \& Regional Director, EMEA 4. Practicum for select applicants 5. Reference checking

title: IMT Flagship Programme Support
company: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
description: **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector. **Job Description** **Role:** IMT Flagship Programme Support **Duration:** 18\-month Fixed Term Contract **Salary:** €47,000 **Reporting to:** Strategic Training Projects Manager **Closing date:** 25 May 2026 at 16:00 All work undertaken within this role will support the overarching principle that patient care remains the priority across all hospital activities and the college is willing and able to support sites to deliver effective training. This role is important for the effective delivery and evaluation of the flagship programme and to ensure no additional pressure is put on BST or local teams with this change. Key Purpose: * Act as the main administrative contact for consultants, trainees, and medical manpower teams involved in the Flagship Internal Medicine Training Programme * Provide administrative support to the management of the Flagship programme * Travel regularly to training sites in Dublin, Galway, and Limerick to build relationships support local programme delivery * Coordinate local reviews local review panels and quarterly meetings * Compile reports of Trainee metrics * Coordinate basic research tasks associated with the programme * Develop strong working relationships with in\-hospital team supporting Trainees * Support the BST operations team * Support the Education Delivery Team in the management of teaching for flagship Trainees Structure of Role * Reporting to Strategic Training Projects Manager * Standing meetings with BST Manager and Team Lead * Provide administrative support directly to programme directors in relation to tasks associated with flag ship programme * This is a hybrid role with an estimate of up to 50% on site activity + This will include same day travel to Limerick and Galway + Candidates should expect at least two days per week in office and this may vary at points in the programme Day to day role * Work as a member of the Internal Medicine Review Project Team * Support the IMT project manager with the delivery of the flagship programme * Lead the coordination, documentation and communication of trainee information * Monitor the frequency and quality of trainee–trainer meetings * Coordinate trainer meetings and programme governance meetings * Maintain an up\-to\-date database of trainees and trainers across the Programme * Support trainee progression and respond to trainee\-related issues or queries. * Coordinate research\-related activities associated with the flagship programme. * Support the organisation of focus groups. * Manage surveys and research data collection. * Liaise with the programme’s research sub\-group. * Support the planning on the 2027 programme intake * Collect and collate programme data for reports and evaluations * Monitor compliance with training standards and accreditation requirements * Assist in organising training events, educational activities, and site visits. * Facilitate communication between training bodies and local sites. * Support initiatives such as educational programmes, examinations, and mentoring activities as required **Requirements** Person Specification * Self\-starter with high motivation, flexibility and the ability to offer a results\-oriented approach with high levels of drive, commitment and enthusiasm * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Excellent stakeholder management skills * Knowledge of project management * Proven track record of achievement in projects administration * Basic research experience * Attention to detail and excellent documentation skills * Ability to work with minimal direction * High personal energy and a positive approach to dealing with challenges, conflict and ambiguity **Competencies:** * Task and Project Management * Problem\-Solving and Result Orientation * Communication and Stakeholder Management * Attention to Detail What We Offer * Flexibility: 35\-hour week hybrid working model. This role will have regular on\-site days in Galway and Limerick, as well as in\-office days. Expect up to 3 in\-person days per week, occasionally less and possibly more at peak points. **Why us?** **Leave:** We offer 21 days annual leave with an additional 3\-4 college days as well as long service leave to all employees. We also provide support for staff with Maternity, Sick, Parents, and Parental leave. **Wellbeing:** An Employee Assistance Programme with Spectrum Life is available to all staff members as well as annual flu vaccine. **Flexible Hybrid working model:** The RCPI offer a flexible 35\-hour\-a\-week hybrid working model for all employees from their start date, giving staff the autonomy to work from home combined with time spent onsite. **Pension:** A defined contribution pension scheme of 1\.5% Employee contribution and RCPI contribution from commencement date with an increase after 18 months **Flexible Benefit:** Pro\-rata €1,500 annual flexible benefit upon completion of a successful probationary period. **Life Assurance:** 4 times your annual salary. **Learning \& Development:** We offer CPD options including study leave and unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning. **Diversity and Inclusion** : RCPI recognises the importance of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We were recently awarded a Silver accreditation from Investors in Diversity. We are an open and inclusive organisation that celebrates and welcomes diversity. We lead initiatives that support, promote and demonstrate a culture of mutual respect, inclusivity, and diversity, as outlined in our Diversity and Inclusion policy. **Accommodations** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is an inclusive employer and in line with our policies you are invited to disclose any reasonable accommodations you require for applying, interviewing or working with RCPI. Please be assured this information will be treated as confidential and not used for the purpose of selection. If you have any queries, please contact Suzann Donnelly at suzanndonnelly@rcpi.ie. **About Us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector.

title: IT & AV Support
description: Role Overview We are seeking a proactive and technically skilled IT \& AV Support Engineer to provide first\-class IT and audiovisual support across the business. This role combines traditional IT support responsibilities with hands\-on management of AV systems used in meeting rooms, events, presentations, and collaboration spaces. The ideal candidate will have strong troubleshooting skills, excellent customer service, and experience supporting both end\-user technology and AV environments. Key Responsibilities IT Support * Provide 1st and 2nd line technical support to staff across hardware, software, and network systems * Install, configure, and maintain laptops, desktops, printers, mobile devices, and peripherals * Support Microsoft 365, Windows, macOS, and common business applications * Manage user accounts, permissions, and onboarding/offboarding processes * Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, connectivity, and system issues * Maintain IT asset registers and documentation * Escalate complex issues to third\-party vendors or senior IT personnel where required * Assist with cybersecurity best practices, updates, patching, and endpoint protection AV Support * Set up and support AV equipment for meetings, conferences, presentations, and events * Troubleshoot and maintain meeting room technology including: + Video conferencing systems + Displays and projectors + Audio systems and microphones + Digital signage + Presentation and collaboration tools * Support platforms such as Microsoft Teams Rooms, Zoom Rooms, and hybrid meeting solutions * Conduct routine checks and preventative maintenance on AV systems * Coordinate with external AV vendors and contractors when needed General * Deliver excellent customer service and user training where required * Respond to support tickets within agreed SLAs * Assist with IT and AV projects, upgrades, and office moves * Maintain a clean, organised, and secure technical environment * Participate in occasional out\-of\-hours support for events or maintenance activities Skills \& Experience Essential * Proven experience in IT support and/or AV support roles * Strong knowledge of: + Windows and Microsoft 365 + Networking fundamentals (Wi\-Fi, TCP/IP, switching) + AV systems and conferencing technologies * Experience with: + Teams Rooms / Zoom Rooms + Meeting room troubleshooting + Hardware deployment and support * Excellent problem\-solving and communication skills * Strong customer service mindset * Ability to prioritise tasks and work independently Desirable * Experience with: + macOS support + Intune, Active Directory, or Azure AD + Digital signage platforms + Crestron, Extron, or Q\-SYS systems * ITIL knowledge or certification * Relevant certifications such as: + CompTIA A\+ + Microsoft certifications + CTS (Certified Technology Specialist) Personal Attributes * Professional and approachable * Calm under pressure * Detail\-oriented and organised * Team player with a proactive attitude * Flexible and willing to support business events when required

title: IT Security Engineer
description: The Mater Private Network is seeking a highly motivated IT Security Engineer to join our Cyber Security Department. **Purpose of role:** The successful candidate will join a dedicated cyber security team and will contribute to managing and improving the organisations cyber security posture through technology implementation and security validation of systems \& architecture. This is a varied role with exposure to a broad range of technologies and systems, so we are looking for an adaptable person with a strong foundation of IT skills and some experience in (or at least a passion for) cyber security. **Required** * 5\+ years' experience in relevant hands\-on security or IT positions (ideally Infrastructure or System Administration) * Good technical knowledge of Enterprise IT environments, Microsoft Entra, M365\. * Good working knowledge of Networking * Clear understanding of secure design architecture and principles. * Ability to carry out technical security assessments. * Analytical thinking and problem\-solving skills * Good all\-around security mindset with a can\-do attitude **Desirable** * Experience with security frameworks (e.g. NIST, ISO27001\) desirable. * Relevant industry certifications, although experience \>certification * Experience leveraging scripting languages (e.g., PowerShell, Python) for automation Job Descriptions available on request. title: IT Service Desk Technician
description: **Overview** At John Sisk \& Son, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. The Service Desk Technician role is responsible for the customer facing and 1 st line support role within the Service Desk, on a day\-to\-day basis and to provide support and feedback to the Management \& team coordinator in the running of the department . The role also ensures that incidents and requests are processed within our service level agreements and resolved to customer satisfaction. **Responsibilities** * Provide 1 st level response to technical software and hardware incidents for all Group users, to agreed service levels both in terms of performance and customer satisfaction. * Liaise with 2 nd level technical support staff to ensure support response times are within agreed limits * Installation and configuration of approved hardware, software and mobile devices * Creating, updating and flagging knowledge base articles as required * Clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner * Keep users informed of call status if requested. * Site attendance as required * Delivers, Tags, sets up and assists in the configuration of end user PC hardware, software and peripherals. * Co\-ordinates timely repair of PC equipment covered by 3 rd party vendor maintenance agreements. * Escalation of issues to the Service Desk Management team **Experience** * Desktop Operating Systems * Basic hardware for PC’s * Basic Active Directory preferred * Basic Office 365 administration * Service Management system **Qualifications** * A\+ certification\-preferred * MCSA/MCITP certification desirable * ITIL Foundation\-preferred **Additional Information** * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave plus additional company days * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion – your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland.

title: IT Systems Administrator (Security-Focused)
description: IT Systems Administrator (Security\-Focused) Role Overview The IT Operations Systems Administrator is responsible for the design, build, support, and maintenance of complex, high\-quality infrastructure solutions, with a strong focus on Microsoft technologies. This role serves as the primary interface between the Cyber Security Project Team and the IT Operations function, ensuring that security\-driven infrastructure initiatives are effectively designed and implemented. The position plays a key role in delivering strategic security projects, enhancing the organisation's security posture, and ensuring alignment with infrastructure standards, operational requirements, and regulatory expectations. Key Responsibilities Infrastructure \& Security Leadership * Act as Technical Lead for cybersecurity projects delivering infrastructure\-related security initiatives in a critical 24x7 operational environment * Design and implement scalable, resilient, and secure infrastructure solutions aligned with industry best practices * Lead the design and delivery of secure architecture aligned to business, regulatory, and operational requirements Systems Administration \& Operations * Perform daily, weekly, and periodic system administration tasks to maintain system health and performance * Manage vulnerability remediation and security patching across infrastructure environments * Monitor infrastructure and security performance to meet SLA requirements * Support high availability and disaster recovery strategies for critical systems Design \& Implementation * Design and build complex Microsoft\-based infrastructure environments with a focus on security hardening * Implement failover strategies and conduct regular resilience testing * Collaborate with application teams to ensure performance, resilience, and secure traffic flows Support \& Continuous Improvement * Resolve escalated technical issues from first\- and second\-line support teams * Provide ongoing recommendations for process improvements and operational efficiencies * Mentor junior team members and title: Junior Buyer
description: **Junior Buyer** ================ Our people make the difference. Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone \- where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It's the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don't just embrace our differences \- we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are \- and that's something we are really proud of. Which is why we've summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. ***This is a 12 month fixed term position.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us:** We've got your back: employee assistance programme and wellbeing initiatives Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay Perks you'll actually use: product discounts Pension Support: We contribute to your future **Belonging at Work**: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you're valued for *you*. **What You'll Be Doing:** * Responsible for core Front of Shop categories range development. * Delivering category sales and margin. * Build, present and deliver strong category strategies. * Manage the end\-to\-end Life Cycle of the product ranges from conception to customer. * Maintain, manage and grow existing supplier relationships. * Build promotional plan for selected categories and communicate these plans to Marketing, Sales \& Ops teams. * Negotiate joint business plans and long\-term agreements with your suppliers. * Identify and execute opportunities to increase revenue and margin, continuously analysing performance and acting where appropriate. * Remain up to date with market and industry trends to update customer offering and review competitor offer and price positioning. * Working with the space planning manager to build and execute planograms. * Prepare for and present range reviews and presentations. * Attend various trade shows to uncover new product opportunities. * Liaise with internal supply chain to ensure optimal delivery of product to store. Conduct regular store visits across the estate to support stores in growing sales. * **What You'll Need to Succeed:** * 2\+ years in buying or category management with demonstrated negotiation skills essential. * Experience in pharmacy retail or other retail chain a distinct advantage * Excellent Microsoft office and reporting skills. * Customer\-oriented approach * Critical thinker and problem\-solving skills **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland's leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland's leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe's leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to jobs@phxireland.ie

title: Junior Consultant - Full Training Provided
description: **Junior Recruitment Consultant – No Recruitment Experience Required!** * **Full Autonomy** – Run your desk like your own business. * **True Ownership** – You control your career and progression. * **Total Clarity** – Performance is the only limiter. **The Role** Access Healthcare is expanding, and we’re looking for motivated professionals to join our team as Recruitment Consultants. If you come from a customer service or client\-facing background and are ready to step into a more rewarding, performance\-driven career, this could be the opportunity for you. At Access Healthcare, we help talented professionals transition into recruitment, where your communication skills, resilience, and people focus directly impact your success and earnings. **What We Offer** * **Lucrative Commission \& Bonus** – your earnings reflect your performance * **Clear and transparent promotion structure** – 12 defined promotions with no blockers other than performance * **Ongoing professional development** – learn sales, negotiation, account management, and become a trusted partner to clients * **Flat meritocracy** – have a real voice, make an impact, and never be “just another number” **Training \& Development** No recruitment experience? No problem. We provide full training designed specifically for professionals transitioning from all backgrounds, including: 1:1 training, team\-based training, and ongoing mentorship. **Your Responsibilities** As a recruiter at Access Healthcare, you will: * Specialise in a niche healthcare market * Have full ownership of your day\-to\-day activity and long\-term career path * Build trusted relationships with clients and candidates * Be accountable for your own success and earnings **What We’re Looking For** * Minimum 1 years’ commercial or customer service experience (customer service, customer success, client services, client experience) * Ability to work office\-based in Dublin city centre (Monday–Friday) * Strong drive to progress and earn * EU Passport or Stamp 4 visa **Average Year 1 Earnings (Base \+ Commission): €47,000** **Base DOE – Commission Uncapped**

title: Junior IT Support Technician
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** We are looking for an IT Support Technician to join our internal IT team and provide first\-line support to staff across multiple offices throughout Ireland. This is a great opportunity for someone with 1\+ years IT support experience who is looking to build their career in a busy, hands\-on environment. The role is focused on day\-to\-day user support, responding to tickets, troubleshooting common IT issues, setting up devices, and helping staff both remotely and on site. You will work closely with the wider IT team and play an important role in keeping users productive and IT services running smoothly. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide first\-line IT support to staff across multiple office locations * Log, triage, and manage incidents and service requests through the IT ticketing system * Support users by email, phone, remote support tools, and in person * Troubleshoot day\-to\-day issues relating to laptops, desktops, printers, mobile phones, tablets, and other end\-user devices * Set up, deploy, and maintain user devices and equipment * Assist with user account administration and general Microsoft 365 / Windows support tasks * Maintain accurate device and asset records * Escalate more complex issues to senior members of the IT team where required * Assist with routine IT maintenance tasks, software updates, and general housekeeping * Support small IT projects and office visits as needed **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * 1\+ years experience in an IT support or service desk role * Good working knowledge of Windows and Microsoft Office / Microsoft 365 * A solid understanding of core IT fundamentals is essential, including IP addressing, DNS and DHCP. Candidates should understand how these technologies enable devices and systems to communicate across a network. * Experience supporting end users with common hardware and software issues * Strong communication skills and a helpful, professional approach * Ability to manage and prioritise workload through a ticketing system * A genuine interest in IT and a willingness to learn and develop * Ability to work well as part of a team and use initiative when required * Full driving licence and own transport, as occasional travel to other office locations may be required. (Travel expenses will be paid per a kilometre travelled) **Desirable** * Exposure to Active Directory, Office 365 administration, basic networking, and remote support tools * Experience with device builds, patching, or asset management * IT certifications such as CompTIA A\+, Network\+, CCNA or Microsoft certifications **What The Role Offers** * A hands\-on opportunity to build broad IT support experience * Exposure to a wide range of technologies and day\-to\-day operational support * The chance to develop your skills within a busy internal IT team * Ongoing learning and practical experience across multiple office environments * A strong culture of continuous learning, with encouragement for team members to pursue relevant certifications and further develop their technical skills **As occasional travel to company locations is required, candidates must hold a full clean driving licence valid for use in Ireland, or be willing to obtain one within the first 6\-9 months of employment.** **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Mechanical Estimator
description: **About Us** Since the 1950s, Leo Lynch has been providing the pharmaceutical, semiconductor, industrial, food, commercial and retail industries with professional project management and high\-calibre craftsmanship. At Leo Lynch, we build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Leo Lynch employs the best people, invests heavily in training and education, and ensures that the highest standards of health, safety and governance are applied throughout the organization. **About The Role** Reporting to the Senior Estimator and as an integral part of the estimating team, you will be responsible for assisting to prepare accurate mechanical cost estimates in a timely manner. You will become proficient in the use of cost estimating software with training provided. You will work on tenders for projects such as Pharmaceuticals, Food \& Beverage, Data Centres, Semi\-Conductor, Commercial, etc across Ireland, UK and Europe. **Responsibilities** You will assist to: * Conduct in\-depth reviews of tender documents, drawings, specifications, schedules, etc * Preform accurate quantity take\-offs and mark up measured drawings * Request, manage and evaluate supplier and sub\-contractor quotations * Issue tender queries and RFIs * Prepare costed bill of quantities * Prepare tender pricing schedules and other tender return documents * Manage data base * Attend meetings and site visits are when required * Liaise with clients, consultants, contractors, and wider project teams * Other related tasks as instructed **Candidate Requirements** * Degree in either Mechanical, Process, Building Services Engineering or other related field * Previous experience in a contracting environment is beneficial but not essential * Strong computer skills especially in the use of MS Office * Understanding of mechanical pipework systems, HVAC, plumbing and other mechanical building services systems * Strong attention to detail * Ability to manage own workload and meet deadlines * Must display excellent time management, communication, numerical, analytical and organisational skills * Problem solving skills with attention to detail and multitasking abilities We know job descriptions can feel overwhelming. So, if you meet most of the above requirements and are excited about this opportunity, we encourage you to apply. We value potential and drive just as much as experience, and every application will be genuinely considered. **Leo Lynch is an equal opportunity employer.**

title: Junior privacy counsel
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * Partner with Product, Engineering, and Security teams to embed privacy\-by\-design principles and translate regulatory requirements into practical technical solutions. * Identify privacy risks and opportunities early, helping shape compliant product features, workflows, and internal processes. * Manage privacy\-related legal work—using AI tools to accelerate drafting and initial review of DPAs, privacy policies, LIAs, and DPIAs, while applying your judgment to validate, adapt, and ensure compliance with global data\-protection regulations. * Conduct privacy audits and coordinate work with external consultants and legal advisors, ensuring alignment across business and technical teams. * Monitor evolving privacy regulations and case law, sharing clear guidance internally to support ongoing compliance and sound decision\-making. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

description: Barden is partnering with an international law firm seeking a Knowledge Lawyer to join the Finance \& Capital Markets department in Dublin. title: Lab Systems Specialist
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. An amazing opportunity has arisen for a **Laboratory Systems Specialist.** This role will support the Testing SDWT/Hub for testing of incoming raw materials, in\-process drug substance/drug product, stability samples and environmental monitoring of the controlled manufacturing areas and utilities areas. **What you will do:** Bring energy, knowledge, innovation and leadership to carry out the following: * The motivation to be an inspiring member of a high performing team. * The desire to continuously learn, improve and develop. * Be the SME on data management systems for instrument control in the laboratories, this includes but is not limited to GLIMS (Global Labware LIMS Platform), Laboratory Execution Systems (LES), Lonza MODA and Waters Empower. * Work within an environment of *right first time* in everything that we do, through a focus on principles of lean, including simplification, standard work, visual management * Build the Lab IT Systems to support Tech Transfer/Commercialisation of the biologics pipeline * An understanding in analytical methodology (e.g. HPLC, ELISA, SDS\-Page, Glyco analytic, Bio\-Assays, PCR, capillary electrophoresis, compendial assays). * A working understanding of GMP, ICH, USP and global compendia regulations and guidance's, particularly as related to analytical method development and validation. * The candidate will have strong problem solving and trouble shooting skills Requirements * Degree qualification in a Technical discipline. * At least 2 years’ experience in laboratory systems, Labware LIMS experience preferred. * Previous experience with configuring, building, maintaining laboratory management systems such as LIMS/ MODA and /or Empower systems and interest in coding/ script. * Ability to think logically and be proactive under pressure. * Ability to work as part of a team and on own initiative in a constructive manner. * Flexible and self\-motivated.

title: Laboratory Technician
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Job Purpose:** The Laboratory Technician will support GMP laboratory operations by ensuring that laboratory facilities, equipment, and consumables are maintained in a compliant, safe, and efficient manner. The role is critical in sustaining laboratory readiness through effective cleaning, inventory management, and adherence to health, safety, and environmental requirements. Requirements **Key Responsibilities:** **Laboratory Support \& Housekeeping:** * Perform glass washing, drying, and appropriate storage of laboratory glassware in compliance with GMP requirements. * Clean and sterilize laboratory equipment, utensils, and work surfaces to maintain a safe and compliant working environment. * Carry out routine cleaning of the Central Testing Unit (CTU) in accordance with approved procedures and schedules. * Maintain 5S standards for laboratory areas, including fridges and freezers, ensuring organization, cleanliness, and clear labelling at all times. **Inventory \& Materials Management** * Monitor laboratory consumables and inventory levels on a routine basis. * Restock laboratory supplies as required to ensure uninterrupted laboratory operations. * Report inventory shortages, expired materials, or damaged items to senior laboratory staff in a timely manner. * Ensure materials are stored according to defined conditions and GMP requirements. **Health, Safety \& Environmental Compliance:** * Strictly adhere to health and safety regulations, including the correct use of personal protective equipment (PPE). * Dispose of hazardous and non\-hazardous laboratory waste in line with site procedures and regulatory requirements. * Immediately report safety concerns, incidents, or near misses in accordance with procedures. * Support a strong safety\-first culture within the laboratory environment. - **GMP \& Quality Compliance:** * Perform all assigned duties in compliance with cGMP, company policies, and site procedures. * Accurately complete required documentation and records in line with data integrity principles. * Support audit and inspection readiness by maintaining laboratory areas at required standards. **Communication \& Teamwork:** * Communicate effectively with laboratory colleagues and senior staff regarding laboratory status, inventory issues, and safety concerns. * Work collaboratively as part of a GMP laboratory team to support daily operations and site objectives. - **Key Skills \& Competencies:** * Attention to Detail: Ability to consistently maintain high standards of cleanliness, organization, and compliance. * Safety Compliance: Strong awareness of laboratory safety, chemical handling, and waste disposal requirements. * Organization: Ability to manage multiple tasks while maintaining orderly laboratory environments. * Communication: Clear and effective communication when reporting issues, shortages, or safety concerns. * Reliability: Demonstrates a dependable and responsible approach to routine laboratory duties. * Teamwork: Works well with others to support laboratory and site goals. **Qualifications \& Experience (Desirable):** * Previous experience working in a laboratory or GMP\-regulated environment is an advantage. * Familiarity with laboratory housekeeping, inventory management, or regulated cleaning activities is beneficial. * Training in health and safety or laboratory practices is desirable; full training will be provided.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: License Key Analyst
description: **The Role** You will be part of the team that manages ION’s license key function, which is crucial in delivering ION solutions to our customers. As a member of the team, you will work internally with key stakeholders (sales, commercial, legal) to ensure correct and timely delivery of the license key, as well as helping to manage customer relationships through addressing and responding to customer queries. **Key Responsibilities** * License key query management, ensuring a min response time of 24 hours * Daily license key management * Preparation of customer pricing simulations and financial analysis * Drafting and reviewing commercial proposals for customers * Dealing directly with customers and senior company management * Analysis of contractual terms in respect of internal and external queries * Assisting with the development of scalable commercial processes * Assisting with the integration of newly acquired companies * Other Ad hoc work as required **Required Skills, Experience And Qualifications** * Commercially astute * Strong critical thinking skills * Attention to detail * Strong analytical skills * Self\-motivated and hard\-working individual who is goal orientated * Demonstrated ability to work under pressure and to deadlines * Intellectually curious * Ability to multi\-task and adapt to a rapidly changing environment * Good interpersonal and communication (verbal and written) skills * Process orientated * Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint * Good to have – B2B / SaaS Sales experience, and Salesforce knowledge * 1\.1 or 2\.1 degree in any field * 2\+ years in experience in a fast\-paced environment **About Us** We’re a diverse group of visionary innovators who provide trading and workflow automation software, high\-value analytics, and strategic consulting to corporations, central banks, financial institutions, and governments. Founded in 1999, we’ve achieved tremendous growth by bringing together some of the best and most successful financial technology companies in the world. * Over 2,000 of the world’s leading corporations, including 50% of the Fortune 500 and 30% of the world’s central banks, trust ION solutions to manage their cash, in\-house banking, commodity supply chain, trading and risk. * Over 800 of the world’s leading banks and broker\-dealers use our electronic trading platforms to operate the world’s financial market infrastructure. With 10,000 employees and offices in more than 40 cities around the globe, ION is a rapidly expanding and dynamic group. At ION, we offer careers that provide many opportunities: To invent. To design. To collaborate. To build. To transform businesses and empower people around the world to do more, faster and better than before. Imagine what you can do and experience. This is where you can do your best work. ION adheres to an equal employment opportunity policy that prohibits discriminatory practices or harassment against applicants or employees based on any legally impermissible factor. Learn more at https://iongroup.com/.

title: M&A Specialist
description: **M\&A Specialist – Boutique Corporate Finance Firm \| Dublin \| Hybrid** HIREIQ is delighted to be partnering with a highly regarded **boutique Corporate Finance firm in Dublin** as they look to appoint an **M\&A Specialist** to join their growing deal advisory team. **Why Apply?** * Excellent opportunity to join a specialist boutique Corporate Finance firm with strong deal flow. * Gain hands\-on exposure across the full M\&A transaction lifecycle. * Work directly with experienced Corporate Finance Directors and Partners. * Strong variety of buy\-side, sell\-side, fundraising and strategic advisory mandates. * More responsibility, client exposure and progression than a larger advisory environment. * Ideal move for someone from Corporate Finance, Transaction Services, Deals Advisory, M\&A, Investment Banking or Restructuring. * Opportunity to work closely with business owners, private equity firms, investors and senior leadership teams. * Competitive salary, bonus potential and strong long\-term career progression. **About the Firm** This is a well\-established boutique Corporate Finance advisory firm with a strong reputation across the Irish market. The firm advises business owners, shareholders, private companies and investors on a range of transactions, including company sales, acquisitions, management buyouts, fundraising, valuations and strategic advisory projects. Due to continued growth and a strong pipeline of mandates, the firm is now seeking to add an ambitious M\&A Specialist to the team. The Opportunity As M\&A Specialist, you will play a key role in supporting the execution of Corporate Finance and M\&A transactions from initial engagement through to completion. This is a highly commercial role suited to someone who enjoys financial analysis, deal execution, client interaction and working in a fast\-paced advisory environment. You will gain excellent exposure to live transactions and have the opportunity to take real ownership across projects. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the execution of buy\-side and sell\-side M\&A transactions. * Prepare financial analysis, valuations and deal\-related reports. * Assist with financial modelling, business plans and investor materials. * Conduct market research, sector analysis and buyer/investor mapping. * Prepare information memorandums, pitch decks and client presentations. * Support due diligence processes and liaise with legal, tax and financial advisers. * Analyse target company financials, trading performance and growth opportunities. * Assist with transaction management, project tracking and deal documentation. * Build relationships with clients, investors, funders and wider professional advisers. **Your Background** * Experience in Corporate Finance, M\&A, Transaction Services, Deal Advisory, Investment Banking, Restructuring or a similar advisory environment. * Strong financial analysis, valuation and commercial assessment skills. * ACA, ACCA, CFA or similar qualification would be beneficial, but not essential. * Strong Excel, financial modelling and presentation skills. * Excellent communication skills with the confidence to engage with clients and senior stakeholders. * Commercially minded with a genuine interest in deals, businesses and the Irish M\&A market. * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple workstreams. * Ambitious, proactive and keen to build a long\-term career in Corporate Finance.

title: Management Accountant
description: **Management Accountant \- North Dublin** **€70,000 – €75,000** We’re currently recruiting on behalf of our client, a well\-established and growing business within the FMCG sector, for an experienced Management Accountant to join their finance team. This is a key role within the business, supporting both finance and operational teams with accurate reporting, commercial insights, and strong cost control. It’s a great opportunity to get real visibility across the business and play a part in ongoing growth. **Key Responsibilities** * Preparation of weekly and monthly management accounts * Reconciliation of key balance sheet items (sales, VAT, prepayments, accruals etc.) * Full ownership of balance sheet accounts, ensuring strong controls are in place * Regular review of accounts with senior stakeholders * Preparation of weekly KPIs and financial reporting * Support with annual budgeting (sales and overheads) * Assist with year\-end audit and liaise with external auditors * Work closely with Accounts Receivable to resolve queries and support cash flow * Investigate pricing issues, deductions, and discrepancies * Monitor stock adjustments and manage financial risk * Support compliance requirements (GDPR and other relevant standards) * Contribute to continuous improvement across finance processes **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified accountant with strong academic background * Ideally 2\+ years PQE * Experience in FMCG or a fast\-paced environment is an advantage * Strong Excel and general IT skills (PowerPoint, Teams etc.) * Good communicator, comfortable dealing with stakeholders across the business * Able to work on own initiative * Team player with a hands\-on approach **What’s on Offer** * **€70,000 – €75,000 salary** * Staff discount * Subsidised canteen * Free on\-site parking (including EV charging) * Wellness initiatives * Strong team environment with good exposure across the business

title: Management Accountant - Insurance
description: A global insurance firm is seeking to appoint a Management Accountant to join its high\-performing finance team. This is a key role within a fast\-paced and expanding organisation, offering excellent exposure across core finance activities and ongoing integration projects following recent acquisitions. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in supporting finance operations, ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting, and driving improvements across processes and systems. The position offers significant interaction with senior stakeholders, providing valuable business partnering experience and the opportunity to influence decision\-making. It's an ideal opportunity for someone looking to step into a commercially focused role within a dynamic and evolving environment, where they can add real value and develop their career. **Key Responsibilities** Financial Reporting \& Month\-End * Prepare monthly management accounts (P\&L \& Balance Sheet) * Perform reconciliations and investigate variances * Deliver accurate and timely reporting to Group * Manage accruals, provisions, and bad debt processes Business Partnering * Partner with senior stakeholders to provide financial insight * Deliver performance analysis and support decision\-making * Contribute to budgeting and forecasting processes Financial Control \& Compliance * Support audit processes and prepare audit schedules * Assist with statutory reporting, VAT, and tax compliance * Maintain strong financial controls and ensure regulatory compliance Projects \& Process Improvement * Support integration of newly acquired entities * Assist with process improvements and system enhancements * Contribute to finance transformation initiatives **Requirements** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA) * 3\+ years' experience in financial services or similar * Strong month\-end and management accounting experience * Proven business partnering skills * Advanced Excel; ERP experience beneficial * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environment **Strong base salary attached.** **How to apply:** Contact Sinéad Canning on 086 800 6785 for more information about this position. You can also apply below with your CV. **About Engage People** Engage People is a specialist recruitment firm in Dublin, Ireland that connects clients and candidates in the Accountancy \& Financial and Banking \& International Financial Services sectors.

title: Marketing Cloud - Account Executive

The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Job Description** **About the Role:** We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Account Executive to join our team and play a pivotal role in driving the success of Salesforce's Agentforce Marketing platform. As a Marketing Cloud Account Executive, you will identify, develop, and close deals with new and existing customers, helping them achieve their agentic marketing transformation goals through the strategic implementation of our Salesforce’s Marketing Cloud solution. **What You’ll Do** * Build trusted relationships with key stakeholders, including C\-suite executives and IT leaders, ensuring long\-term value and growth. * Lead the end\-to\-end sales cycle, from prospecting to closing, while collaborating with internal teams, such as Sales Engineers, Partners, and Professional Services. * Develop comprehensive account strategies aligned with customer business objectives, driving revenue and ensuring satisfaction. * Represent Salesforce at industry events, showcasing our vision for the future of our agentic innovations. **What We’re Looking For** * Proven success in SaaS sales * Excellent stakeholder management, both internally and externally * Continuous learning mindset, to stay ahead of the latest innovations * Growth mindset, demonstrated in both nurturing the install based customers and opening up new logos * Demonstrated ability to perform in a fast\-paced, target\-driven environment. * Expertise in managing complex sales cycles and crafting compelling value propositions. * Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship\-building skills, particularly with C\-suite executives. * Passion for innovation, teamwork, and driving transformational change. **Why Join Salesforce** * Innovative Culture: Be part of a company that's reshaping the future with advanced AI models and cloud solutions * Global Impact: Contribute to products that are transforming industries worldwide * Career Growth: Access to professional development opportunities, mentorship programs, and a clear path for advancement * Benefits \& Perks: Check out our benefits site which explains our various benefits, including well\-being reimbursement, generous parental leave, adoption title: Medical Laboratory Scientist - Near Patient Testing (NPT)
description: **Purpose of the Role:** The purpose of this post is to assist in the delivery of NPT services in CHI Temple St., CHI Crumlin (including support to CHI UCC at Connolly and Tallaght) with the ultimate aim of achieving compliance with ISO 22870 Near Patient Testing Requirements for Quality and Competence, and ISO 15189:2012 Medical Laboratories \- Particular Requirements For Quality And Competence standards. **Essential Criteria:** Candidates must, on the latest date for receiving completed application forms for the post, possess: * Bachelor in Science (Applied Science) Honours degree (Biomedical option) from the Technological University Dublin, or previously DIT Kevin Street or * Bachelor in Science Honours degree in Biomedical Science from University College Cork – Cork Institute of Technology course or * Diploma in Medical Laboratory Sciences of the Dublin Institute of Technology, Kevin Street of the Cork Institute of Technology awarded prior to 1994 or * Possess Certificate in Medical Laboratory Sciences of the Dublin Institute of Technology. Kevin Street or Cork Institute of Technology or the Galway/ Mayo Institute of Technology (awarded prior to 1997\) or * A recognised qualification at least equivalent to the above and * Be a member, or be eligible for membership, of the Academy of Clinical Science and Laboratory Medicine (ACSLM) And * Be CORU Registered **How to Apply and Informal Enquiries:** Applications for this post must be accompanied by a cover letter, setting out relevant experience that illustrates how the essential criteria listed above is met. The criterion for short listing is based on the requirements of the post, as outlined in the eligibility criteria. * Please note that you must submit a cover letter with your CV, this forms part of your application and CV’s will not be accepted without a detailed cover letter. The closing date for submissions of CV’s and cover letter is **Monday 1st June 2026 at 11\.45pm.** Applications must be completed through the advertised post on **CHI.jobs** by clicking **‘Apply for Job’.** **Applications will not be accepted through direct email or any other method.** For informal enquiries for this specialty/department, please contact Martina Ring at martina.ring@childrenshealthireland.ie For other queries relating to this recruitment process, please contact Talent Acquisition Rachel.sheridan@childrenshealthireland.ie **PLEASE NOTE:** CHI has transitioned to a process of a one commencement day per month for all new employees, CHI internal transfers and Secondments. This update to our Onboarding process is aligned to changes in our monthly/fortnightly payroll and with the launch of our new corporate induction program. This process enhancement ensures that we can thoroughly prepare for your arrival and facilitate a smooth transition in your onboarding journey. **It is important for you to note that if you do not have your pre\-employments and mandatory training completed in time, your commencement date will be deferred to the next available date.** Below, you’ll find the list of commencement dates for 2026\. * 10th August * 7th September * 5th October * 2ndNovember * 7th December

title: Mid-Market AI Business Partner - French Speaking
description: We are seeking an intellectually curious Mid\-Market AI Business Partner to work directly with Mid\-Market customers. This role is focused on engaging senior business leaders (often FTSE 250 \& Fortune 500 companies) in **high\-value, consultative conversations around AI transformation, data strategy, and business value creation** . This is not a traditional SDR or sales development role. Instead, it is a **strategic discovery and advisory position** , responsible for shaping early\-stage executive dialogue, influencing account strategy, and identifying where AI can unlock meaningful business outcomes. The role focuses on quality over volume, driving well‑researched consultative conversations, identifying the problems end customers are trying to resolve with AI and the use cases \& value the business are hoping to unlock. You will own the early to mid\-sales cycle; from targeted prospecting, account mapping, deep account research through to qualification and warm handoffs. Acting as the first impression and the face of the brand to executive buyers (up to c\-level), you will demonstrate strong executive presence, clear storytelling, and a deep understanding of AI and SaaS solutions to help identify opportunities for the end customer. Success in this role comes from a curious mindset to perform deep discovery, technical competency, \& a passion in AI and its application within a business. **Key Responsibilities** * Prospect and qualify new business opportunities through thoughtful, targeted outreach. * Lead executive level discovery conversations (c\-suite/c\-1\). Engage C‑suite and senior stakeholders with clear, confident, and consultative communication. * Shape account strategy, not just pipeline. This includes mapping complex organisations and stakeholders * Act as a thought partner to mid\-market customers through driving deep, structured discovery to uncover root business problems, challenge assumptions and connect technical capability with business value. * Partner closely with Account Directors on account planning \& development, research, and meeting preparation. Identify opportunities and build strategic hypotheses on where AI can deliver value * Own qualification and handoff, ensuring Account Directors receive well‑defined, high‑quality opportunities. * Maintain accurate CRM data, forecasting, and activity records. * Continuously build AI \& market expertise. Stay current on AI, SaaS, and market trends to communicate complex concepts clearly. **Basic Qualifications** * Completed High School Diploma (Irish Leaving cert or equivalent) * English C1 and French C1 * Minimum 1\-2 years' B2B sales experience **Qualifications** * 3\+ years’ experience in tech sales, inside sales, or business development (AI/SaaS/enterprise preferred). * A strong background/experience with Account Planning and Development * Strong technical competency, curiosity and ability to simplify complex topics. Background in AI, data, SaaS or digital transformation preferred * Exceptional storytelling \& communication skills * Curiosity \& problem solving to take structured approaches to discovery \& hypothesis building * Professional presence to face into executive levels * Detail‑oriented, research‑driven, and experienced with Salesforce or similar CRM tools.

title: Named Account Executive

Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.
